impact_evidence: Environmental | Air & Climate | Energy demand and consumption -> "These articles contend that the alleged subsidies are to blame for Zambian State-owned power utility Zesco's long- term failure to invest in power infrastructure and additional generation capacity." || Governance | Reputation & Social License | Mining image and public perception -> "Claims that the mines are being subsidised have the unfortunate effect of portraying the mining industry as being responsible for Zambia's power deficit and Zesco's precarious financial situation." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "The power deficit in Zambia goes beyond the sole issue of tariffs, and includes other factors, such as regulation, competition and the operational efficiency of Zesco,"

title: To stop losing mining revenues, dig the details | Oxfam
impact_evidence: Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "there is currently no legal requirement in Zambia for the maintenance of transfer pricing documentation. This oversight is a significant obstacle to tackling transfer mispricing since government officials do not have the basic information they need to monitor and evaluate related party transactions." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> "Without this basic information, the Zambia Revenue Authority is unable to monitor risky transactions and identify potential transfer mispricing." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Corporate transparency and reporting -> "Governments around the world commonly require transfer pricing documentation from companies so they must maintain, if not regularly submit, timely and accurate information about their related party transactions." | "require transfer pricing documentation in the forthcoming regulations ("statutory instruments") to the Income Tax Act." || Governance | Stakeholder Engagement & Rights | Stakeholder inclusion in decision-making -> "the protocol for mineral valuation procedures between agencies and the agency with ultimate jurisdiction over the matter is still unclear." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "Corporate tax avoidance deprives governments of desperately needed tax income for the provision of public goods and services forcing governments to choose between cutting public services or collecting additional tax from citizens." || Social | Living Conditions & Social Fabric | Livability (cost of living, food security, communication services) -> "citizens face eroding hospitals and schools and struggle to pay higher prices for basic goods." || Social | Equity, Rights & Vulnerable Groups | Wealth distribution and inequality -> "preventing aggressive tax avoidance is all the more critical for reducing inequality."

title: Zambia : KCM directed to stop the transfer of 1,000 employees to its contractors
impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Employment creation and job security -> "Therefore, there will be no job losses as a consequence of outsourcing and all employees would continue to enjoy full KCM conditions of service." | "KCM remains committed to working with all the stakeholders including Government, Labour Unions and all employees in ensuring sustained security of employment." || Governance | Stakeholder Engagement & Rights | Stakeholder inclusion in decision-making -> "The Ministry advised that any issues bordering on the transfer of unionised workers to the contractors be discussed with the unions in line with their various recognition agreements." | "Government wants to stress that it is the unions that negotiate with the employer, and Government only comes in when the unions upscale the matter to the Ministry of Labour and Social Security where there is an impasse." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Mining permitting and licensing practices -> "Of course outsourcing is not illegal but KCM can only outsource if the contractor has an employment licence and abides by the provisions of section 56 of the Employment Amendment Act No. 15 of 2015 which deals with employment agency permits and this can only be done after negotiations between management and the unions."

impact_level3: Employment creation and job security || Stakeholder inclusion in decision-making || Mining permitting and licensing practices

impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "government' s loss of revenue due to tax evasion, avoidance and illicit financial outflows that the real " Gold mine" for domestic revenue mobilization which is the Mines and Multinational corporations pay their fair share of revenue..." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "there is a very urgent need for legislation on the Extractive industry and Commercial Illicit financial outflows with should have severe
